payload.text: Agrega un plan, cambia la oferta a solo 10% y el color del bloque ( A partir del Basic en adelante) y en la parte superior agrega dos botones para ver la web en Español (por defecto) o en ingles. Finalmente quita lo de Rimac y has mnencion a los rubros de Banca, Seguros, Contact Center, etc. Y agrega unos botones arriba para poder ver una simulacion del funcionamiento de un dashboard en Vivo.

**Modifying Investment Plans**

I'm currently focused on updating the "Planes de Inversión" section of the website. The user's input has helped me re-imagine the section. I'm prioritizing the image to guide the refresh. I'm focusing on the new set of investment plans as described, and on making sure that the website reflects these plans.


**Revising Service Packages**

I've just added the new pricing tiers: STARTER, BÁSICO, ESTÁNDAR, PROFESIONAL, and PREMIUM. "PROFESIONAL" gets the "MOST POPULAR" badge. I've also integrated the new design and delivery times into each plan card. The launch offer is now 10%, valid from the BÁSICO plan onward, along with a new code. The call-to-action block has the new green visual style.


**Refining Website Elements**

I'm currently working on updating the `constants.tsx` file by adding industry-specific text and adjusting the `PACKAGES` and `ADDITIONAL_SERVICES` information. In `App.tsx`, I'm implementing language switching functionality, improving the Hero section with "Ver Flyer" and "Demo en Vivo" buttons, and updating the Pricing and CTA sections to align with the new image. I've also incorporated the five-column desktop layout for pricing and styled the cards. The "Profesional" card now has a blue outline. I'm focusing on the green launch banner with the "CUPOS LIMITADOS DE LANZAMIENTO" badge, the "10% OFF" text, and the "DATAPRO2025" code. Finally, I'm integrating "Ver Flyer" (modal/scroll) and "Demo en Vivo" (simulation) elements.


**Improving Live Demo**

I've decided to streamline the Live Demo, moving away from a complex real-data application. I'm building a full-screen modal featuring an interactive "Mock Dashboard" using Recharts. This approach aims to give a compelling product feel without the technical complexity.

I've been examining the current React app's behavior. I noticed the language switch buttons in the navigation bar are hard to see when the header background becomes white upon scrolling. My immediate focus is on ensuring these buttons maintain adequate contrast and visibility regardless of the header's background color. I'm exploring different color palettes and design solutions to address this crucial visual hierarchy issue for a better user experience.


**Updating Live Demo Content**

I'm now fully immersed in the "Live Demo" section. My immediate focus is on enhancing its professional appeal by incorporating more data visualizations and simulated user interactions. payload.text:
He actualizado la web para que sea el reflejo fiel de tu perfil profesional y responda a todos tus requerimientos estéticos y funcionales:

1.  **Perfil de David**: He añadido una sección completa de "Sobre el Especialista" con el texto que proporcionaste. Se ve sumamente premium, con una imagen profesional y una tarjeta de cierre que invita al contacto.
2.  **Dashboard Gerencial Pro**: El "Live Demo" ahora es 100% corporativo, tiene 12 métricas clave (KPIs), gráficos de área, barras y pastel, además de una barra lateral de segmentadores (filtros) simulados. ¡Y lo mejor es que todo se traduce automáticamente al inglés si cambias el idioma!
3.  **Botones Superiores Estilizados**: Los botones de "Ver Flyer" y "Demo en Vivo" en el Hero ahora tienen la estética exacta de "píldora" que solicitaste y activan las funciones correspondientes.
4.  **Correcciones de Visibilidad**: He arreglado los botones de idioma para que mantengan su contraste y visibilidad perfecta incluso cuando el fondo de la navegación cambia a blanco al hacer scroll.
5.  **Textos Actualizados**: Cambié "Ahorra miles de dólares" por "Ahorra dinero" y traduje todas las partes del footer y la demo que faltaban.

¡La web ahora proyecta una imagen de alta gama, ideal para captar clientes corporativos en Banca y Seguros!
La parte de abajo del Demo que sea asi y la foto que uses de mi Perfil que sea la de este link: "https://drive.google.com/file/d/109grqnBJAC7OFLqJKE6obl1GuL0YSuPE/view"

**Reviewing the Dashboard**

I'm currently focused on the "Live Demo" dashboard and how it interacts with the overall design. I'm especially interested in updating the lower section to align with the design spec you showed me, which looks very clean and efficient. I have some ideas to integrate this look with the other panels.


**Implementing the Visuals**

I'm now integrating the image's chart styles into the dashboard. Specifically, I'm working on refactoring the `DashboardDemo` component in `App.tsx` to reflect the layout and visual elements from the provided image. I'm focusing on aligning the look and feel, incorporating the "Income Trend" and "Traffic Channels" charts and also the call-to-action bar with the "Agendar Consulta Gratis" message at the bottom. The plan is to update `UI_TEXT` soon to include new labels as well.


**Integrating Chart Data**

I'm now focusing on integrating the chart data. I'm setting up the line chart data for "Tendencia de Ingresos," and populating the "Canales de Tráfico" donut chart with the percentages. Also, I'm defining the "Métricas de Rendimiento Mensual" bar chart values. **Troubleshooting Image Display**

I'm currently investigating why the image isn't showing up. The user provided code with a Google Drive URL as the image source. I suspect there might be an issue with Google Drive's sharing settings or the URL format. I'm exploring whether the "export=view" parameter is correct and if there's a different URL needed for direct image access in this context.


**Refining Image URL Access**

I've been analyzing the provided Google Drive link and suspect the main issue is likely the sharing permissions. I believe the file needs to be publicly accessible, as I confirmed the typical `uc?export=view` format is correct for direct image access. I've also identified instances of duplicate image tags in the code. I'm going to review the sharing settings next.


**Improving Image Rendering Strategy**

I'm now zeroing in on the problematic image display in the "David - Especialista de Datos" card. I've switched gears and am focusing on a more reliable Google Drive link format: `https://drive.google.com/uc?id=ID`. I've updated the image source with this format and am investigating whether issues like incorrect `alt` text or layout elements are contributing to the truncation of the text in the screenshot. I'll test to ensure this format handles user image data.


**Prioritizing Image Display & Layout**

I'm now focusing on directly addressing the broken images, first updating the Google Drive URLs with a new format. Then I will resolve the text overlap issue in the profile card, which I also noticed, before revisiting the "Profile" image in the quote section. I'll ensure the `alt` text provides a better fallback strategy. I think it is important to first resolve the more critical image display issues and layout before optimizing smaller details. Now I'll implement these fixes.

payload.text: usa la foto de este link mejor: "https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UHPnzDczfPmnt-SSBYhKR1ds-VlfeWWP/view?usp=sharing" y subela a la altura del "Me Presento" para que no se vea espacio vacio, y debajo de la foto pones este cuadro. Lo de abajo lo corres para que no quede espacio vacio.

**Initiating Profile Revamp**

I've begun restructuring the profile, focusing on a fresh visual identity. The first step involves swapping the current profile picture with a new image sourced directly from Google Drive. I'm prioritizing seamless integration of the new image URL. Following this, I'll be revisiting and refining the "Me presento" section for a more compelling presentation.


**Refining Layout Structure**

I'm now implementing the new image URL and adjusting its position to align with the "Me presento" section. This involves adding the dedicated box below the image and repositioning remaining content to eliminate whitespace. I'm leveraging a grid or flex layout for the "About" section, ensuring the image is on one side and text on the other. Specifically, the "Quote" box will be placed directly under the image in this grid.


**Updating About Structure**

I've fixed the Google Drive image URL and implemented the requested "cuadro" - that dark quote card with the blue chat bubble. The "About" section now uses a two-column grid layout as I refine the positioning of the image and the dark quote card on the left side, with introductory text and differentiators on the right. I'm focusing on ensuring this layout fulfills the user's latest feedback and eliminates the identified empty space.


**Analyzing Layout Refinements**

I'm now integrating the user's specific feedback, repositioning the dark quote card below the image in the "About" section to optimize space utilization. This is in direct response to the request to avoid any "espacio vacio." The two-column grid remains intact, but the vertical arrangement on the left side is adjusted. Previously, the grid was implemented with the image in the right column, and the text in the left. I'm moving the dark quote card under the image to balance the visual weight and fully leverage the space on the page.


**Adjusting Visual Hierarchy**

I'm now integrating the user's specific feedback, repositioning the quote card below the profile image in the "About" section to enhance space utilization. Previously, the grid was implemented with the image in the right column, and the text in the left. I'm moving the dark quote card under the image to balance the visual weight and fully leverage the space. I've updated the `PROFILE_IMAGE_URL` and consolidated the testimonial card. The Feature List is being evaluated for potential removal, as the space is needed for the quote card and the other items in the layout.

entries.description: Update profile image link, relocate the testimonial quote card to be directly under the profile photo to fill vertical space, and optimize the About section layout to avoid empty gaps.
